rulings.Case Title: HSBC BANK USA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION AS TRUSTEE FOR VS. KEYHAN MOHANNA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 16, 2021 line 1. PLAINTIFF HSBC BANK USA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION AS TRUSTEE'S FOR MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES: June 1, 2021 Declaration of Emilie Edling is stricken. This declaration was not responsive to the Court's request for supplemental briefing. Additionally, this declaration is not executed under penalty of perjury of the laws of the state of California. Unauthorized June 2, 2021 Response to Declaration in Opposition is stricken. The Court further notes that no courtesy copies of June 1 and June 2, 2021 filings were provided to the Court in violation of SFRL 2.7B. April 1, 2021 unauthorized filing of declaration of Keyhan Mohana is stricken. Motion is DENIED. Assuming that this action was an action on the DOT, the moving party failed to establish with admissible evidence its entitlement to recover fees incurred by Specialized Loan Servicing or support the reasonableness of the billings by all individuals referenced in the invoices. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ROSE FELIX VS. 925-945 CORBETT HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement, And An Order Barring And Dismissing All Cross-Actions, Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 877.6(A)(1), 877.6(C)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 16, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANT WATER DAMAGE RECOVERY OF CALIFORNIA, INC.'S Amended Notice Of Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement, And An Order Barring And Dismissing All Cross-Actions, Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 877.6(A)(1), 877.6(C) is DENIED. This motion is untimely. CRC compliant Notice of Motion was filed on June 14, 2021 for June 16, 2021 hearing. To the extent moving party attempted to address the deficiency in the original Notice of motion pointed out in opposition, no authority is cited for the proposition that such amendment in procedurally proper. If the Court were to address this motion on the merits, the motion would be denied. Opposing party has successfully shown that the amount of settlement is not in the ball park of this defendant's proportional liability for the damages claimed by Plaintiff and the moving party failed to address some of the Tech-Built factors altogether. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: OASIS GRILL, LLC ET AL VS. ERIC KETCHUM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION / Second Amended Notice Of Motion & Motion For Summary Adjudication That Oasis Grill Breached The Lease Contract By Not Paying Rent
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 16, 2021 line 4. DEFENDANT ERIC KETCHUM'S AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION THAT OASIS GRILL BREACHED THE LEASE CONTRACT BY NOT PAYING RENT is DENIED. Multiple triable issue of fact exists as to the payment of rent by the tenant under the circumstances of this case. Furthermore, this motion is procedurally defective. As the moving party concedes in reply Notice of Motion contained no indication what cause of action or affirmative defense is subject to adjudication. This information does not appear anywhere in the notice and is for the first time referred to in the Points and Authorities (Reply 9:27-10:2). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
